item.title: Parents of teens tragically killed by methanol poisoning team up with government for schoolies abroad warning
item.description: The mothers of two Melbourne teenagers who tragically died last year have teamed up with the government in a warning campaign for young travellers.

item.title: New mRNA flu vaccine far more effective than existing jabs, but has some limitations, study finds
item.description: A newly developed mRNA-based flu vaccine is almost 35 per cent more effective against the most common form of influenza, according to a new study.

item.title: ‘The zombies have evolved’: Interview with Resident Evil Requiem producer
item.description: Capcom, the developers of the classic Resident Evil franchise, are gearing up for their ninth major entry in the long running series — Resident Evil Requiem. 9news.com.au caught up with the game’s producer.

item.title: 'Double-edged sword': How Aussie content creators are reacting to the under-16 social media ban
item.description: A﻿s the under-16 social media ban inches closer, questions are being raised about the implications the ban will have on people who target their content toward younger audiences.

item.title: Everything we know about ChatGPT's 'erotica' rollout in December
item.description: Experts from a range of fields, including OpenAI's own wellbeing panel, are worried about what it means for mental health.

item.title: Review: The best game on the Switch 2 yet
item.description: It meets (and arguably exceeds) the exceptionally high bar set by its predecessors and is willing to gamble the franchise's small but loyal fanbase on a few big changes.

item.title: How Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora's From the Ashes expansion aligns with the new film
item.description: Developed in collaboration between Massive Entertainment and Lightstorm Entertainment, the From the Ashes game expansion runs side by side to the events of Avatar: Fire and Ash.

item.title: Apple issues urgent iPhone update after triple zero emergency call testing raises concerns
item.description: Apple has released an emergency update to iOS 26 that applies only to the iPhone 12, addressing a compatibility issue with Australia's emergency calling system.

item.title: The Oscars to abandon broadcast TV, leaving longtime home of US network ABC
item.description: In a seismic shift for one of television’s marquee events, the Academy Awards will depart broadcast TV.

item.title: The TVs of tomorrow: Ultra-thin designs and 130-inch monsters
item.description: CES, the biggest technology show in the world, is happening this week in Las Vegas, a place for the biggest brands and the newest startups to demonstrate their innovation to the world in the hope of making it into retail stores this year.

item.title: 'One Battle After Another' dominates SAG's Actor Awards with seven nominations
item.description: One Battle After Another dominated nominations to the Actor Awards, with Paul Thomas Anderson's ragtag revolutionary saga landing a record seven nods in the annual SAG-AFTRA honours.

item.title: Australian technology companies making it big on the world stage
item.description: A﻿longside the tech giants at the CES trade show in Las Vegas are Australian start-ups displaying their products.

item.title: Australia's most valuable company continues climb after claiming crown from Commonwealth Bank
item.description: Mining giant BHP has consolidated its new crown as Australia's most valuable company, a day after knocking off the country's biggest bank at the top of the stock exchange.

item.title: Gold price soars to $US5300, as questions surround whether Aussie dollar rally will continue
item.description: E﻿xpects are not ruling out the precious metal hitting the $US6000 mark later this year.

item.title: The biggest video games set to be released this year
item.description: 2026 is going to be see some incredible video games released - from the highly anticipated Grand Theft Auto...

item.title: The three things that could save Aussies from looming interest rate hell
item.description: There's some hope for mortgage-holders, one of the big banks has said.

item.title: Tesla's sales slowdown puts Elon Musk's enormous pay deal at risk
item.description: Tesla has seen its first decline in annual revenue in the company's history, putting CEO Elon Musk's historic pay package deal at risk.

item.title: 'Cold shower' for mortgage holders: Experts' gloomy rates' forecast
item.description: E﻿conomists are backing the RBA to raise the cash rate next week, and any future relief could be months away.

item.title: Nine sells talkback radio assets to Laundy family for $56 million
item.description: An Aussie pub tycoon is making a venture into the world of the media with a big purchase.

item.title: Dragging Melbourne roadworks threaten to force locals out of business
item.description: Rebekah Becsi fears she'll have to close down her discount food store in a matter of weeks.

item.title: Gold price tumbles after Trump names pick to lead US Federal Reserve
item.description: I﻿nvestors are spurning gold and other precious metals in a vote of confidence for the man expected to lead the world's most powerful central bank.

item.title: Treasurer defends government spending ahead of expected rates hike
item.description: Mortgage holders are bracing for a rates hike today, but Jim Chalmers insists the government isn't to blame.

item.title: Iconic Sydney brewery to shut its doors
item.description: A popular brewery is set to shut its doors ﻿in Sydney's inner west in search of a tree change.

item.title: 'Silent strain': More than a million homeowners struggle with mortgage repayments
item.description: The latest cash rate rise is another hammer blow for struggling mortgage holders, new research has found.

item.title: We got an early look at three of the biggest games coming to Switch 2. This is our verdict
item.description: Here's what you need to know about Mario Tennis Fever, Pragmata and Resident Evil Requiem.

item.title: Seven years on, Fallout 76 developers still listening to fans and now watching the TV series
item.description: Seven years after its launch, video game Fallout 76 continues to evolve, with new updates shaped by both fan feedback and inspiration from Amazon's Fallout TV series.

item.title: Rio Tinto scraps $300 billion merger plan with rival Glencore
item.description: B﻿oth companies said any potential deal would fail to deliver shareholders enough value.

item.title: Australian luxury retailer Cosette set to close down after more than 11 years in business
item.description: An Australian luxury retailer is set to close down after more than 11 years in business.

item.title: Deal reached to save collapsed Healthscope private hospitals
item.description: H﻿ealthscope will become a not-for-profit operator in order to save its suite of hospitals after the company collapsed financially last year.

item.title: Australia quietly moves to impose tariffs on Chinese steel imports
item.description: T﻿he move follows concerns by local businesses they are facing unfair competition, but risks retaliation from Beijing.

item.title: Create your own Pokemon paradise with the latest in Nintendo life-sim
item.description: After an hour rebuilding my own deserted island back from the brink, it's safe to say our favourite pocket monsters remain irresistibly charming.

item.title: ANZ posts nearly $2 billion in quarterly profit, days after charging staff for 'welcome back' sausage sizzle
item.description: ANZ﻿'s cash profit for the first quarter jumped by six per cent to $1.94 billion, partly driven by the thousands of redundancies announced last year.

item.title: Iconic Australian retailer Barbeques Galore collapses into receivership﻿, 500 jobs at risk
item.description: Around 500 jobs are now at risk.

item.title: Australia Post wants to raise stamp prices
item.description: T﻿he national postal service says it's needed to offset losses in its letter division.

item.title: New God of War game headlines 'epic' PlayStation State of Play event
item.description: A new God of War game, exciting trailers, and several major reveals — PlayStation's February State of Play was one of the strongest showcases in recent memory.

item.title: NSW to get an extra public holiday for the next two years after Anzac Day, Premier confirms
item.description: T﻿he decision hasn't been universally popular, but Minns insists the decision is the right thing to do.

item.title: Higher GST, lower income tax, part of plan to lift Australia's economy
item.description: Strong economic medicine is needed over d﻿ebt levels racked up by Australian states, a global body warns.

item.title: Australian food court chain Sushi Sushi sells to Japanese dining empire in estimated $160 million deal
item.description: Sushi Sushi, which began in Melbourne in 1998, has been snapped ﻿up by Tokyo-based Genki Global Dining Concepts.

item.title: Australian gas producer Santos wins court fight over net zero claims
item.description: A lawsuit against gas producer Santos alleged the company misled the public on its plans to achieve net zero carbon emissions.

item.title: Record number of cafes and restaurants going out of business
item.description: T﻿he hospitality industry in Australia is now split between winners and losers, new research shows.

item.title: 'This is peak Australia': NAB chief warns living standards will stagnate without serious reform
item.description: After generations of improvement, our living standards will be stuck in the present – or go backwards – without major economic reform.

item.title: Business busted for making false claims about link to Bondi terror attack
item.description: N﻿SW Fair Trading found the company falsely claimed the proceeds of sales would go to victims and their families.

item.title: Telstra records billion dollar profit, reveals it shed 2356 jobs in 2025
item.description: T﻿he job cuts have saved $118 million alone.

item.title: eBay to buy Depop in $1.7b move to reach Gen Z and Millennials
item.description: e﻿Bay hopes buying Depop will help it reach a younger and more fashion-forward audience.

item.title: How a blood test could predict if and when you could get Alzheimer's
item.description: A﻿lzheimer's is the biggest cause of dementia, which is the leading cause of death in Australia.

item.title: Australians aren't eating enough burritos. It just cost one company hundreds of millions of dollars
item.description: I﻿ts share price has dropped by more than half in the last 12 months.

item.title: Employees at Aussie start-up Eucalyptus set to receive windfall after $1.6 billion acquisition
item.description: Employee shareholders at an Aussie start-up are set for a big pay day after the company was acquired for $1.6 billion.

item.title: WiseTech Global set to cut up to 2000 jobs for 'AI efficiency'
item.description: T﻿he software company said ongoing developments in AI would deliver a leaner organisation.

item.title: Domino's Pizza sheds 10 per cent of customers after reducing discounts
item.description: Domino's started offering fewer discounts, and some of its customers weren't too happy.

item.title: Man fired after pouring cooking oil down the drain outside popular bakery
item.description: The incident quickly gained attention, with members of the community saying the man's actions were "pure laziness", "disgusting", and ﻿"such a shame".

item.title: Samsung's new phone feature blocks prying eyes
item.description: Samsung's flagship smartphone for 2026 will launch with an industry-first privacy feature aimed at stopping those prying eyes around you from glimpsing what you're looking at on your screen.

item.title: Glue Store to close in shake-up by retail giant
item.description: The company said it now wants to focus on its global brands.

item.title: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese defiant as Australian children detained in Syria plead to come home
item.description: Anthony Albanese has voiced compassion for the 23 Australian children detained in Syria, but remained defiant against their desperate pleas to return home.

item.title: Qantas unveils major change to frequent flyer program as airline records bumper $1.46 billion profit
item.description: Qantas has announced major changes to its frequent flyer program after posting a bumper $1.46 billion half-year profit.

item.title: Sussan Ley's parting shot to Angus Taylor as she leaves politics after 25 years
item.description: In a statement on Friday morning, Ley announced she had tendered her resignation, effective immediately﻿.﻿

item.title: Company behind Australian cultural icon Hills Hoist could soon be sold
item.description: The company behind one of Australia's most iconic brands is set to be put up for sale as its American owner focuses on operations closer to home.

item.title: Almost 40,000 Budget Direct customers allegedly overcharged $3.3 million
item.description: Australia's financial watchdog has taken legal action against the insurer behind the Budget Direct products.

item.title: Government won't rule out changes to negative gearing
item.description: Jim Chalmers has effectively confirmed Treasury has looked into the impact of limiting one of Australia's most contentious tax breaks for property investors.

item.title: Former US president Bill Clinton had no knowledge of Epstein crimes, says Hillary Clinton
item.description: The former US secretary of state and first lady was standing by her husband Bill Clinton at the end of a six-hour deposition.

item.title: VB pins hopes on mid-strength as Aussies drop classic beers
item.description: T﻿he newly-unveiled mid-strength brew signals a growing trend across Australia as illustrated by the sales numbers.

item.title: Man charged with allegedly planning terrorist attack on multiple locations in Perth
item.description: A 20-year-old man has been charged after allegedly planning a terrorist attack on multiple political and religious sites in Perth.

item.title: 'Will not be mourned': PM's brutal message after confirmed death of Iranian leader
item.description: Albanese said he supported the United States "acting to prevent Iran from obtaining a nuclear weapon and to prevent Iran continuing to threaten international peace.

item.title: Dire shortage of healthcare providers capable of performing potentially life-changing endometriosis scans
item.description: A life-changing ultrasound saved her from that fate, but there's a dire shortage of people capable of performing them in Australia.

item.title: The timeline of events leading up to the US-Israeli strikes on Iran and the death of the supreme leader
item.description: T﻿his weekend's strikes by the United States and Israel on Israel come on the back of months of tensions ratcheting up in the Middle East. But the origins go back decades.

item.title: 'Seriously bad advice': British and Irish visitors warned against abusing system by applying for protection vi
item.description: The Department of Home Affairs was forced to issue a public warning against temporary visitors applying for ﻿a $50 protection visa (subclass 866).

item.title: Who's running Iran now that the supreme leader is dead?
item.description: T﻿hese are the top contenders to fill the leadership void in the aftermath of Khamenei's death.

item.title: Dubai was seen as a safe, rich, tax-free oasis. Iranian airstrikes may have just changed that forever
item.description: A﻿n expert has said there's no going back for the city, which was until recently seen as a safe, rich, tax-free oasis.

item.title: Queensland adds another 12 offences to 'adult crime, adult time'
item.description: The government says the 12 offences, mostly domestic and sexual violence, were recommended by the Expert Legal Panel.﻿﻿

item.title: Several security breaches spotted in Donald Trump's situation room during US strikes on Iran
item.description: S﻿everal images were posted online by The White House's social media team.

item.title: Chris Minns flags future plans after revealing when he will retire from politics
item.description: "Well, I won't be here, at least in this role."

item.title: Pauline Hanson slaps wrist and storms out of parliament over censure
item.description: T﻿he One Nation leader slapped her wrist as her colleagues condemned her comments about Muslims.

item.title: Australia's gender pay gap is creeping down, but some major Australian employers are lagging
item.description: Many major Australian brands are failing to turn the dial on the gender pay gap, with some even slipping backwards in the past year, a detailed new report has revealed.

item.title: What are Victoria's proposed work from home laws?
item.description: Under the proposed laws, Victorians who are able to do their job from home will have the right to do so at least two days per week.

item.title: Aussie pubs, jobs safe for now as UK company lays off hundreds of staff
item.description: The company has four breweries and about 100 pubs around the globe, including five here in Australia.

item.title: Iranian drone hits US consulate grounds in Dubai
item.description: The strike was confirmed by US Secretary of State Marco Rubio.

item.title: Cracks appear in Trump's MAGA base as leading figures criticise the Iran war
item.description: For US President Donald Trump, some of the sharpest criticism he's faced in the early days of the Iran war has come from once-loyal media figures far more accustomed to singing his praises.

item.title: Apple unveils $899 MacBook Neo in surprise launch
item.description: It's a brand new model for the tech giant, and it's a big shift away from the typical price point.

item.title: Son of ousted king positions himself to take over in Iran
item.description: He hasn't set foot in the country since he was 18, but there are growing calls to install him as leader.

item.title: Farrer byelection to be held on May 9 after Sussan Ley's resignation from politics
item.description: The byelection will be held on Saturday, May 9, House Speaker Milton Dick announced during Question Time today.

item.title: Australia's most trusted and distrusted brands revealed as local favourites dominate
item.description: S﻿ome brands soared and others struggled.

item.title: Business owner sends premier $24,000 bill after new public holiday announced
item.description: "I'm not arguing against a public holiday, but we were given four months' notice, so we never budgeted for this in our costings."

item.title: Prime minister confirms Australian 'military assets' deployed to Middle East amid Iran war
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese confirmed the deployment in parliament today, following a historic address by his Canadian counterpart.

item.title: Indonesia will ban social media for children under 16, communication minister says
item.description: The implementation will start gradually in the south-east Asian nation from March 28.

item.title: Aldi to build billion-dollar distribution centre at Western Sydney Airport
item.description: T﻿he government claims it will create jobs even though it is set to be mostly automated.

item.title: Former Liberal MP Rory Amon found not guilty on most child sex charges, jury deadlocked on two counts
item.description: A former MP accused of sexually assaulting a teen in a car park toilet has been left in limbo after a jury could not reach a verdict on all charges.

item.title: Australian markets suffer $90 billion wipeout as experts issue stark warning
item.description: At one stage today, the ASX had sunk by as much as 4.3 per cent, which would have put it on track for the steepest single-day drop since the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020.

item.title: Apple's latest iPhone is designed for those clinging onto older phones
item.description: A lot of people using older model phones are contemplating an upgrade.

item.title: Bigfoot, trombone, orca among the latest emojis
item.description: Have you ever wanted to send a text about orcas or Bigfoot or trombones only to find you have to do it using words instead of pictures?

item.title: 'I'm buggered': David Littleproud quits as federal Nationals leader
item.description: A tearful David Littleproud has announced he is resigning as the leader of the federal National Party in a shock development for the junior Coalition party.

item.title: Major superannuation changes to become law, with a push for further tax reform later this year
item.description: The federal government's proposed changes to the way superannuation is taxed will soon become law.

item.title: Apple MacBook Neo review: The laptop market is redefined
item.description: That's AUD, not USD. How does the laptop market survive in its current form when the MacBook Neo exists?

item.title: Lululemon fined more than $700,000 over marketing emails
item.description: A﻿CMA found more than 370,000 emails the company sent were in breach of Australian law.

item.title: MP unveils bold tax plan to give every Australian an extra $1643 a year
item.description: The federal government has been urged to slash income tax in a bold proposal that would see 14.7 million working Australians $1643 richer.

item.title: Matt Canavan wins vote to become new Nationals leader
item.description: Outspoken Queensland senator Matt Canavan has been elected the new leader of the Nationals following David Littleproud's shock resignation yesterday.

item.title: South Australia Liberal candidate under fire for controversial comments labelling feminism, homosexuality 'dem
item.description: ﻿A Liberal candidate in South Australia's state election has come under fire for controversial comments calling feminism and homosexuality "demonic".

item.title: The government granted Iranian footballers asylum. Then, it moved to block 7000 Iranians from Australia
item.description: T﻿he government introduced proposed changes just hours after granting Iranian footballers asylum in Australia.

item.description: "Where is the fuel gone? Is it the cartels just hoarding it?"

item.title: Aussie software giant Atlassian cuts 1600 jobs in push to AI
item.description: The CEO, one of Australia's richest men, said employees would get an email "within 20 minutes" to find out if they had lost their jobs.

item.title: Australia's No.1 retailer named as last year's winner plunges
item.description: T﻿he new king of brick-and-mortar retailers has been decided.

item.title: Twist for man accused of threatening to kidnap Prime Minister Anthony Albanese
item.description: An allegation that a man threatened serious harm against the prime minister in an online post has been dropped by prosecutors.

item.title: 'Un-Australian': Energy Minister Chris Bowen warns against re-selling petrol on Facebook Marketplace for profi
item.description: Drivers in Australia have been seen stockpiling fuel as petrol and diesel prices soar in response to the conflict in the Middle East.﻿

item.title: Man in intensive care after brawl at Melbourne pizza shop
item.description: A Melbourne man is in intensive care after a brawl broke out at a pizza shop in front of shocked diners.

item.description: T﻿he cost of the visa doubled overnight this month.

item.description: Hollywood actress Theresa Saldana was stabbed by an obsessed stalker on March 15, 1982.

item.description: The team were branded "traitors" by the Islamic regime when they refused to sing the Iranian national anthem before a game in the Asia Cup.

item.description: "﻿[It was] the biggest surprise I had of this whole thing."

item.title: New laws planned to crack down on Victoria's tobacco wars
item.description: The Victorian government is planning to introduce new laws to try and extinguish Melbourne's tobacco war, which include powers to forcibly shut down dodgy retailers.

item.title: One Nation surges in the polls, stripping potential votes from Labor and the Coalition
item.description: The federal opposition fell to a record low of 22 per cent.

item.description: As the firebrand Queenslander strips voters from Coalition and Labor, we take a deep dive into her politics.

item.title: Two dead, 11 hospitalised in UK university meningitis outbreak
item.description: T﻿wo people have died in a meningitis outbreak in the UK with another 11 people in hospital.

item.title: Male shoe anxiety hits the Oval Office
item.description: In the Oval Office, a stylistic choice caught the public's eye this week: photos of Marco Rubio and JD Vance show them wearing black dress shoes with visible gaps between the shoe's collar and the wearer's foot.

item.description: The 98th Academy Awards are here, celebrating the best films and performances of the past year.

item.title: Iran hits Gulf neighbours and keeps stranglehold on oil shipping as concerns rise of energy crisis
item.description: Explosions echoed across Beirut early on Monday as Israel struck the Lebanese capital. It also launched a new wave of attacks on Tehran, while Dubai was forced to temporarily close its airport after an Iranian drone hit a fuel tank.

item.title: Three weeks in, there's one big winner from the war in Iran: Vladimir Putin
item.description: Vladimir Putin is enjoying spoils from a war launched by Russia's long-time adversary against one of its closest allies.

item.description: The crowd in the room audibly groaned at the revelation.

item.description: He did not specify whether his ambitions would require military action including ground troops.﻿

item.description: It comes as major fuel companies have been hauled in front of the consumer watchdog for a please-explain.

item.description: "There are hundreds of thousands of people who don't use their firearms for the reason on their licence."

item.description: The owner said it was a "sad and difficult" decision to appoint administrators. ﻿

item.title: Airline warns against customer service scam targeting travellers on social media
item.description: The low-cost carrier today warned travellers after online posts listing fake customer service numbers circulated on social media.

item.description: "She wanted to leave Eric Richins but did not want to leave his money."

item.title: Strikes escalate in Lebanon and Iraq, UAE briefly closes airspace, Chalmers blames war for inflation driving u
item.description: Inflation in Australia has not been immune to the conflict either, with the Reserve Bank's decision to raise the cash rate to 4.10 per cent blamed partly on the ongoing war.﻿

item.title: Israel says it killed two top Iranian officials in wartime blow to country's leadership
item.description: It's a major blow to the country's leadership in a time of war.

item.title: Today in History - March 18: With a note from his father, he conducted the biggest bank heist of all time
item.description: The son of a notorious dictator carried out the biggest bank heist of all time on March 18, 2003.

item.title: Trump-appointed intelligence official resigns over Iran war, says Iran posed 'no imminent threat'
item.description: A Trump-appointed US intelligence official has resigned over the Iran war, claiming Iran posed "no imminent threat".

item.title: Trump berates Australia, other allies as he finds himself alone on Iran war
item.description: For the last 80 years, every American military action has come with the backing of many of its allies - including Australia.

item.title: Government drops clearest hints yet controversial tax break to be wound back
item.description: The federal government has dropped its two clearest hints to date it is preparing to wind back the capital gains tax (CGT) discount for property investors in the upcoming budget.

item.title: Australian troops safe after Iranian missile strikes near base in the UAE
item.description: "The Iranian regime are engaging in frankly random attacks across the Gulf region."

item.title: Australian man accused of assaulting TSA officer at Los Angeles airport
item.description: T﻿he man allegedly pulled the hair of a TSA officer and threw him against a glass wall.

item.description: The prime minister revealed the "random" attack happened only hours ago.

item.title: 'I don't accept it': Kyle Sandilands reveals contract terminated by ARN
item.description: T﻿he shock jock said ARN "decided to try and burn the place down" over an on-air spat with Jackie O.

item.title: Both sides in Iran war ratchet up attacks on energy facilities, as oil prices surge
item.description: An offshore natural gas field has been struck in a sign of the war's mounting pressure - from both sides - on the region's economic lifeblood: energy.

item.title: Today in History - March 19: Astronauts had to battle for survival after spacecraft lands in deeply remote for
item.description: Two Russian cosmonauts had to battle for survival after landing in remote Siberia after a historic spaceflight on March 19, 1965.

item.title: 'Not the Australian way': PM's blunt message for hoarders ahead of national cabinet meeting on fuel supply
item.description: The prime minister will talk to state premiers and chief ministers today in a national cabinet meeting aimed at ensuring Australia has a united response to the global oil supply shock.

item.title: Australia and dozens of others condemn Iran over attacks on neighbours, critical shipping closure
item.description: Australia has joined with Gulf countries and several of its closest allies to condemn Iran's attacks against its neighbours and lockdown of a critical shipping channel in the Middle East.

item.title: Cheese pulled from shelves at Coles, IGA over undeclared allergen
item.description: An urgent product recall has been issued for a "dairy-free" cheese sold nationally at Coles supermarkets and independent retailers such as IGA.

item.description: N﻿early 20 per cent of Aussies actively avoid going to the CBD.

item.description: JD Vance has told an audience other countries were "suffering" on petrol prices worse than America.

item.description: T﻿wo people have died and at least 20 cases have been reported.

item.title: Watchdog to investigate major fuel companies over diesel availability in regional Australia
item.description: The consumer watchdog has announced an investigation into allegations of anti-competitive conduct by major fuel suppliers in regional and rural Australia.

item.description: I﻿t seems to have debunked a common belief about the 'designer' crossbreed.

item.description: Iran was not planning a sneak attack or working on a nuclear weapon, Donald Trump's former counterterrorism chief has said.

item.description: Flight enthusiasts are set to be impacted by the changes.

item.title: How loophole allowed longshot politician to win election with profanity directed at Trump
item.description: A US politician has won an upset election victory on the back of a simple but very effective message: "F— Trump".

item.title: Notorious outback murderer's death probed in public hearing
item.description: Notorious outback killer Bradley John Murdoch died in hospital under the watch of prison guards in July.

item.title: Is Australia headed for a recession? The latest job data paints a grim picture
item.description: The latest employment figures has now stoked fresh fears that Australia's economy could be shocked into a recession for the second time in six years.

item.title: 'I've done things': Mayor makes wild admissions in interview about alleged assault
item.description: "Any 60-year-old who lives in Fitzroy, who says they've never taken a line of cocaine, or smoked a joint, I'm telling you, they're lying to your face."

item.description: "The offender exploited a special relationship."

item.title: Sydney rock musician faces possible life behind bars for alleged involvement in $5.5m international drug ring
item.description: Police have charged eight people, including the musician Azaria Byrne, who is accused of running an international drug ring out of a music studio in Sydney's inner west.

item.title: Victorians warned after outbreak of severe respiratory disease
item.description: Six cases of Legionnaires' disease have been recorded in Craigieburn and surrounding northern suburbs since early March.

item.title: Crime rises to record levels in Victoria as embattled Premier Jacinta Allan dismisses leadership speculation
item.description: T﻿he concerning data comes as Jacinta Allan insists speculation about a leadership coup is "anonymous speculation".

item.title: Rising fuel costs putting pressure on Australia's seafood industry
item.description: Rising fuel costs are putting fishermen under pressure, with fears the price increase could impact Easter seafood.﻿

item.title: Albanese makes plea to Australians over petrol as new task force announced
item.description: A﻿lbanese's message came as he insisted Australia did not have a fuel supply issue.

item.title: Victim advocate slams ex-Olympic cyclist Rohan Dennis' Instagram post of car as 'deeply offensive' after wife'
item.description: Dennis, 35, was handed a suspended jail term in May last year after his wife Melissa Hoskins, 32, was fatally struck by his vehicle near their home in Adelaide.

item.title: Millions in Australia could pay less on power bills in the next financial year
item.description: S﻿ome households could save nearly a thousand dollars per year on their power bills.

item.title: Crisis escalates as petrol stations across NSW run out of fuel
item.description: Across the state, 42 petrol stations are﻿ reportedly out of fuel, and almost double have no diesel.﻿﻿

item.title: Treasurer delivers grim forecast as he touts 'ambitious' tax reform
item.description: J﻿im Chalmers said the volatile global environment was the reason for bold reform.

item.title: Teen charged after woman stabbed inside Melbourne shopping centre
item.description: A 25-year-old woman was walking to work just before ﻿8am when she was allegedly stabbed by an unknown male.

item.title: Homicide squad investigates woman's death in Perth home
item.description: Police rushed to a home on Mayali Bend in Banksia Grove after receiving a report ﻿of an unresponsive woman about 9am.

item.title: Iran hits back at multiple Gulf refineries after Israeli strike on its offshore gas field
item.description: Iran has intensified its attacks on its Gulf Arab neighbours' energy sites, hitting a Saudi refinery on the Red Sea and setting Qatari liquefied natural gas facilities and two Kuwaiti oil refineries ablaze.

item.title: 'I can't sugarcoat this': Premier's final warnings as Cyclone Narelle﻿ strengthens to category 5
item.description: It's expected to be the strongest system to cross this part of the Far North Queensland coast in a century.

item.title: Perth man with seatbelt exemption handed almost $20,000 in AI fines
item.description: The state government today encouraged motorists to appeal fines they didn't agree, after 40,000 were issued in five months.

item.title: Australia falls to new low in World Happiness Rankings
item.description: "Recent evidence suggests that younger individuals... are experiencing declining levels of wellbeing and mental health."

item.title: Aussies warned not to panic as medicine shortages, price hikes caused by war in Iran loom
item.description: The conflict in the Middle East is causing ﻿logistical issues and supply shortages.

item.title: One Nation could give Labor and the Coalition a 'major scare' during the South Australia election
item.description: An orange wave is expected to crash over rural South Australia as voters head to the polls tomorrow.

item.title: Experts warn of 50 per cent food price spike as fuel crisis deepens
item.description: Australians are being warned to brace for a massive surge in food prices as the escalating conflict in the Middle East chokes global oil supplies.

item.title: Today in History - March 20: Princess Anne tells would-be kidnapper 'not bloody likely'
item.description: Four people were shot in a botched kidnapping attempt on Princess Anne on March 20, 1974.

item.title: Court challenges on the horizon as AI seatbelt fines soar in Perth
item.description: Hundreds of WA motorists who argue their fines are unfair are preparing to take their cases to court.

item.title: Student charged after allegedly firing airgun at another student at Sydney school
item.description: Police alleged he showed another student what appeared to be a firearm before allegedly firing a pellet at him.

item.title: Missile lands metres from reporter crossing live on Russian TV from Lebanon
item.description: They'd been reporting on the destruction of a bridge in southern Lebanon.

item.title: Teen fighting for life after falling from moving train in Sydney's south
item.description: He was taken to ﻿hospital last night in a critical condition.

item.title: Trump compares Iran attack to Pearl Harbor in front of Japan's PM
item.description: Donald Trump has defended his surprise attack on Iran by comparing it to Pearl Harbor while he sat next to Japan's prime minister.

item.title: More than five million Aussies to receive cash boost from today
item.description: H﻿alf of those set to benefit are pensioners.

item.title: SA Decides 2026: Have your say on the key issues
item.description: South Australia goes to the polls on Saturday and as part of our 9News special coverage we want to hear your views on the key issues.

item.title: Mystery lotto winner will soon be $100,000 richer - they just need to come forward
item.description: T﻿hey just need to come forward and collect the prize.

item.title: Police appeal for information after man found dead in Melbourne river
item.description: The man was discovered in the ﻿Maribyrnong River in Melbourne's north-west last month.

item.title: CCTV of terrifying home invasion released as masked men storm house with baseball bat, gun
item.description: Police were later told the unknown men had threatened the people in the home, demanding money and drugs before taking multiple items and escaping on an e-bike.

item.title: Denmark sent blood, explosives to Greenland in case US invaded: reports
item.description: The stunning new revelation shows just how close two firm allies came to war.

item.title: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese heckled, protester thrown out after commotion at Lakemba Mosque
item.description: Protesters came forward in the crowd to yell calls of "shame" and "disgrace".

item.title: Date set for first step in cop killer's sentencing
item.description: Constable Keith Smith, 57, was killed at North Motton, in northwest Tasmania, on June 16 while he and another officer were delivering a court-ordered repossession notice.

item.title: Three crew members hospitalised after Sydney-bound Delta flight hits turbulence
item.description: Three crew members have been hospitalised after they were launched into the air when a Delta flight from Los Angeles to Sydney hit turbulence this morning.

item.title: Australia urged to crack down on blind boxes due to gambling fears
item.description: Experts believe blind boxes use all the same tactics and produce the same chemicals linked to gambling.

item.title: Manhunt underway after 71-year-old randomly stabbed during morning walk in Melbourne
item.description: Despite ﻿a thorough search of the area, police have been unable to locate the offender.

item.title: Hammer blow for Aussies as oil crisis to send cost of flying domestic sky high
item.description: It follows price rises at the bowser, as well as for flying international, as the Middle East crisis intens...

item.title: Father asked if son 'wanted to be red' before killing him in attempted murder-suicide
item.description: The 48-year-old took the child into the bathroom of his aunt's Sydney apartment in May 2023, one-and-a-half hours after learning his partner had made a domestic violence complaint to police about him.

item.title: Cyclone weaves a trail of destruction
item.description: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle has started to cross the far north Queensland coast. The category 4 system ...

item.title: Virgin hikes domestic airfares by 5 per cent due to rising oil prices
item.description: Q﻿antas and Jetstar similarly increased fares for the same reason.

item.title: Entertainment news headlines - 9News
item.description: Breaking entertainment news including celebrity gossip, film and movie news, music news, award ceremonies, scandals, photos and videos

item.title: Latest technology news headlines, updates & new tech innovation | 9News
item.description: Latest technology news stories including consumer electronics, science and space technology, gadgets, internet tech, medical devices, websites and social tech, cryptocurrencies and other technology news headlines

item.title: Latest health news top stories & analysis
item.description: Latest health news, research and results, scientific discoveries, diets, obesity, cancer, medical procedures and medicine, hospitals, and government health guidelines

item.title: Business news headlines - 9News
item.description: Latest business news, company tax, government regulations, CEOs, entrepreneurs, customers, and big business strategy

item.title: Politics News - 9News - Latest political news today Australia and World
item.description: Latest political news from Australia and around the world, covering elections, by-elections, our regular leadership spills, poll results, Question Time debates and daily Australian party politics

item.title: World news headlines and international current affairs - 9News
item.description: Latest world news headlines: International breaking news and current affairs from US, Europe, Middle East, Asia, Africa and more. If it happens around the world, you will know about it on 9News

item.title: News Australia - Latest news headlines in Australia
item.description: Today's news in major cities, regional and local areas which can include accident reports, police & emergency responses, criminal and court proceedings or live breaking news
